channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Bengaluru food delivery startup Swish raises $38M: its third round in 18 months
item.description: Swish has more than doubled its valuation in a year as its full-stack, hyperlocal model positions ultra-fast food delivery as a high-frequency habit.

item.title: I want to love Marathon but can already see why players are falling off its miserable grind
item.description: Marathon looks great and has some strong moment-to-moment action, but it lacks compelling reasons to keep playing.

item.title: Despite bitter rivalry, Kalshi, Polymarket CEOs back $35M predictions markets VC fund
item.description: As prediction markets explode, the new firm called 5(c) Capital, will back startups supporting the burgeoning category.

item.title: Gas Prices Are So High That DoorDash Is Providing ‘Emergency Relief’
item.description: The platform will give cash back on gas for its drivers.

item.title: The US government just banned consumer routers made outside the US
item.description: In December, the Federal Communications Commission banned all future drones made in foreign countries from being imported into the United States, unless or until their maker gets an exemption. Now, the FCC has done the exact same for consumer networking gear, citing "an unacceptable risk to the national security of the United States and to […]

item.title: FCC Bans All New Routers Not Made in America
item.description: Routers the FCC has already approved can still be sold, but further approvals of foreign-made routers will not be issued.

item.title: Air Street becomes one of the largest solo VCs in Europe with $232M fund
item.description: London’s Air Street Capital has raised a large Fund III with eyes locked on backing early-stage European and North American AI companies.

item.title: The US bans all new foreign-made network routers
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission has released a notice today designating any consumer routers manufactured outside the US as a security risk. The rule states that new foreign-made product models for network routers will land on the Covered List, a set of communications equipment seen as having an unacceptable risk to national security. Previously purchased routers can still be used and retailers can still sell models that were approved by the prior FCC policies. In an exception to the usual rule, routers included on the Covered List can continue to receive updates at least through March 1...

item.title: Apple Maps may be about to get ads
item.description: Apple Maps may soon serve ads in top results when users search on terms like "restaurants."

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg Is Building an AI CEO. Will He Lay Himself Off?
item.description: After the metaverse fiasco, maybe it would be for the best.

item.title: This lightweight and portable Lenovo Chromebook is now 70% off
item.description: The Lenovo Chromebook 100E Gen 2 is on sale for $60, a 70% discount on a compact laptop for daily use and online work.

item.title: Hackers says they breached Crunchyroll, stole nearly 7 million users data
item.description: Crunchyroll is investigating a potential breach after a hacker made shocking claims.

item.title: The Next PlayStation May Rely on AI More Than Any Console Before Before It
item.description: Get ready for "fake frames" on the PlayStation 6, or whatever Sony is cooking up next.
